steps.title: Identify the signal
steps.content: The team's conclusion is that “certain microbes in the soil might explain the difference” between healthy and diseased plants. To directly support this, I need evidence showing which specific microbes differ between healthy and diseased plants.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly shows that healthy plants have significantly higher concentrations of microbes known to inhibit the disease-causing bacterium—the microbial difference that explains their protection.
  • B contradicts this by showing disease bacteria are present equally regardless of plant health
  • D contradicts it by showing disease happens regardless of microbial differences
  • C discusses moisture instead of microbes
. That matches choice A — A is correct.
